signature: {m : ℕ} (hm : m ≥ 1) (c : ℝ) : offDiag_sum (m:=m) (fun _ _ ↦ c) = m * (m - 1) * c

name: offDiag_sum_add
signature: {m : ℕ} (f g : Fin m → Fin m → ℝ) : offDiag_sum (fun j k ↦ f j k + g j k) = offDiag_sum f + offDiag_sum g

name: offDiag_sum_sub
signature: {m : ℕ} (f g : Fin m → Fin m → ℝ) : offDiag_sum (fun j k ↦ f j k - g j k) = offDiag_sum f - offDiag_sum g

name: offDiag_sum_div
signature: {m : ℕ} (f : Fin m → Fin m → ℝ) (c : ℝ) : offDiag_sum (fun j k ↦ f j k / c) = offDiag_sum f / c

name: offDiag_mul_sum
signature: {m : ℕ} (f : Fin m → Fin m → ℝ) (c : ℝ) : offDiag_sum (fun j k ↦ c * f j k) = c * offDiag_sum f

name: offDiag_sum_le
signature: {m : ℕ} (f g : Fin m → Fin m → ℝ) (h : ∀ j k, j ≠ k → f j k ≤ g j k) : offDiag_sum f ≤ offDiag_sum g

name: multidist_ruzsa_I_indep
signature: {m : ℕ} (hm : m ≥ 1) {Ω : Type*} (hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ.volume)] (X : Fin m → Ω → G) (h_indep : iIndepFun X) (hmes : ∀ j, Measurable (X j)) (hfin : ∀ j, FiniteRange (X j)) : ∑ j, ∑ k, (if j = k then (0:ℝ) else d[X j # -X k]) ≤ m * (m - 1) * D[X; fun _ ↦ hΩ]
docstring:
To prove multidist_ruzsa_I, we first establish a special case when the random variables are
defined on the same space and are jointly independent.

name: multidist_ruzsa_I
signature: {m : ℕ} (hm : m ≥ 1) {Ω : Fin m → Type*} (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) {X : ∀ i, Ω i → G} (hmes : ∀ j, Measurable (X j)) (hprob : ∀ j, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ j).volume) (hfin : ∀ j, FiniteRange (X j)) : ∑ j, ∑ k, (if j = k then (0:ℝ) else d[X j # -X k]) ≤ m * (m - 1) * D[X; hΩ]
docstring:
Let `m ≥ 1`, and let `X_[m]` be a tuple of `G`-valued random variables. Then
  `∑ (1 ≤ j, k ≤ m, j ≠ k), d[X_j; -X_k] ≤ m(m - 1) D[X_[m]].`

name: multidist_ruzsa_II
signature: {m : ℕ} (hm : m ≥ 2) {Ω : Fin m → Type*} (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) (hprob : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ i).volume) (X : ∀ i, Ω i → G) (hmes : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (hfin : ∀ i, FiniteRange (X i)) : ∑ j, d[X j # X j] ≤ 2 * m * D[X; hΩ]
docstring:
Let `m ≥ 2`, and let `X_[m]` be a tuple of `G`-valued random variables. Then
  `∑ j, d[X_j;X_j] ≤ 2 m D[X_[m]]`.

name: multidist_ruzsa_III'
signature: {m : ℕ} (hm : m ≥ 2) {Ω : Type*} {hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω} {X : Fin (m + 1) → Ω → G} (hmes : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (hident : ∀ j k, IdentDistrib (X j) (X k)) (hindep : iIndepFun X) (hfin : ∀ i, FiniteRange (X i)) [IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : Measure Ω)] (i₀ : Fin m) : D[Fin.init X ; fun _ ↦ hΩ] ≤ m * d[X i₀.castSucc # X i₀.castSucc]
docstring: A version of multidist_ruzsa_III assuming independence.

name: multidist_ruzsa_III
signature: {m : ℕ} (hm : m ≥ 2) {Ω : Fin m → Type*} (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) (X : ∀ i, Ω i → G) (hident : ∀ j k, IdentDistrib (X j) (X k)) (hmes : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (hprob : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ i).volume) (hfin : ∀ i, FiniteRange (X i)) (i₀ : Fin m) : D[X; hΩ] ≤ m * d[X i₀ # X i₀]
docstring:
Let `I` be an indexing set of size `m ≥ 2`, and let `X_[m]` be a tuple of `G`-valued random
variables. If the `X_i` all have the same distribution, then `D[X_[m]] ≤ m d[X_i;X_i]` for any
`1 ≤ i ≤ m`.

name: multidist_ruzsa_IV
signature: {m : ℕ} (hm : m ≥ 2) {Ω : Type u} [MeasureSpace Ω] (X : Fin m → Ω → G) (h_indep : iIndepFun X) (hmes : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) [IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : Measure Ω)] (hfin : ∀ i, FiniteRange (X i)) : d[∑ i, X i # - ∑ i, X i] ≤ 2 * D[X; fun _ ↦ inferInstance]
docstring:
Let `m ≥ 2`, and let `X_[m]` be a tuple of `G`-valued random
variables. Let `W := ∑ X_i`. Then `d[W;-W] ≤ 2 D[X_i]`.

name: multidist_eq_zero
signature: [Finite G] {m : ℕ} (hm : m ≥ 2) {Ω : Fin m → Type*} (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) (hprob : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ i).volume) (X : ∀ i, Ω i → G) (hvanish : D[X; hΩ] = 0) (hmes : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (hfin : ∀ i, FiniteRange (X i)) (i) : ∃ (H : AddSubgroup G) (U : Ω i → G), Measurable U ∧ IsUniform H U ∧ d[X i # U] = 0
docstring:
If `D[X_[m]]=0`, then for each `i ∈ I` there is a finite subgroup `H_i ≤ G` such that
`d[X_i; U_{H_i}] = 0`.

name: condMultiDist
signature: {m : ℕ} {Ω : Fin m → Type*} (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) {S : Type*} [Fintype S] (X : ∀ i, Ω i → G) (Y : ∀ i, Ω i → S) : ℝ

name: condMultiDist_of_inj
signature: {G : Type*} [hG : MeasurableSpace G] [AddCommGroup G] {m : ℕ} {Ω : Fin m → Type*} (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) {S : Type*} [Fintype S] {T : Type*} [Fintype T] (X : ∀ i, Ω i → G) (Y : ∀ i, Ω i → S) {f : S → T} (hf : Injective f) : D[X | fun i ↦ f ∘ (Y i); hΩ] = D[X | fun i ↦ Y i; hΩ]
docstring: Conditional multidistance is unchanged if we apply an injection to the conditioned variables

name: condMultiDist_of_const
signature: {G : Type*} [hG : MeasurableSpace G] [AddCommGroup G] {m : ℕ} {Ω : Fin m → Type*} [hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)] [∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ i).volume] {S : Type*} [Fintype S] (c : Fin m → S) (X : ∀ i, Ω i → G) : D[X | fun i ↦ fun _ ↦ c i; hΩ] = D[X ; hΩ]
docstring: Conditional multidistance against a constant is just multidistance

name: condMultiDist_nonneg
signature: [Finite G] {m : ℕ} {Ω : Fin m → Type*} (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) (hprob : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : Measure (Ω i))) {S : Type*} [Fintype S] (X : ∀ i, Ω i → G) (Y : ∀ i, Ω i → S) (hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) : 0 ≤ D[X | Y; hΩ]
docstring: Conditional multidistance is nonnegative.

name: prod_mul
signature: {α β : Type*} [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] [CommMonoid β] (f : α → β) (c : β) (i₀ : α) : (∏ i, f i) * c = ∏ i, if i = i₀ then f i * c else f i
docstring: A technical lemma: can push a constant into a product at a specific term

name: mes_of_comap
signature: {Ω S G : Type*} [hG : MeasurableSpace G] [hS : MeasurableSpace S] {X : Ω → G} {Y : Ω → S} {s : Set S} (hs : MeasurableSet s) : MeasurableSet[(hG.prod hS).comap fun ω ↦ (X ω, Y ω)] (Y ⁻¹' s)

name: ident_of_cond_of_indep
signature: {G : Type*} [hG : MeasurableSpace G] [MeasurableSingletonClass G] [Countable G] {m : ℕ} {Ω : Type*} [hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω] {S : Type*} [Finite S] [hS : MeasurableSpace S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] {X : Fin m → Ω → G} (hX : (i : Fin m) → Measurable (X i)) {Y : Fin m → Ω → S} (hY : (i : Fin m) → Measurable (Y i)) (h_indep : iIndepFun (fun i ↦ ⟨X i, Y i⟩)) (y : Fin m → S) (i : Fin m) (hy : ∀ i, ℙ (Y i ⁻¹' {y i}) ≠ 0) : IdentDistrib (X i) (X i) (cond ℙ (Y i ⁻¹' {y i})) (cond ℙ (⋂ i, Y i ⁻¹' {y i}))
docstring:
A technical lemma: two different ways of conditioning independent variables gives identical
distributions

name: prob_nonzero_of_prod_prob_nonzero
signature: {m : ℕ} {Ω : Type*} [hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω] {S : Type*} [Finite S] [MeasurableSpace S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] {Y : Fin m → Ω → S} {y : Fin m → S} (hf : ∏ i, (ℙ (Y i ⁻¹' {y i})).toReal ≠ 0) : ∀ i, ℙ (Y i ⁻¹' {y i}) ≠ 0
docstring:
A technical lemma: if a product of probabilities is nonzero, then each probability is
individually non-zero

name: condMultiDist_eq
signature: {m : ℕ} {Ω : Type*} [hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω] {S : Type*} [Fintype S] [hS : MeasurableSpace S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] {X : Fin m → Ω → G} (hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) {Y : Fin m → Ω → S} (hY : ∀ i, Measurable (Y i)) (h_indep : iIndepFun (fun i ↦ ⟨X i, Y i⟩)) : D[X | Y ; fun _ ↦ hΩ] = H[fun ω ↦ ∑ i, X i ω | fun ω ↦ (fun i ↦ Y i ω)] - (∑ i, H[X i | Y i])/m
docstring:
If `(X_i, Y_i)`, `1 ≤ i ≤ m` are independent, then
`D[X_[m] | Y_[m]] = H[∑ i, X_i | (Y_1, ..., Y_m)] - 1/m * ∑ i, H[X_i | Y_i]`

name: condMultiDist_eq'
signature: {m : ℕ} {Ω : Type*} [hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω] {S : Type*} [Fintype S] [hS : MeasurableSpace S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] {X : Fin m → Ω → G} (hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) {Y : Fin m → Ω → S} (hY : ∀ i, Measurable (Y i)) (h_indep : iIndepFun (fun i ↦ ⟨X i, Y i⟩)) : D[X | Y ; fun _ ↦ hΩ] = ∑ y : Fin m → S, (ℙ (⋂ i, (Y i) ⁻¹' {y i})).toReal * D[X; fun _ ↦ ⟨cond ℙ (⋂ i, Y i ⁻¹' {y i})⟩]
docstring:
If `(X_i, Y_i)`, `1 ≤ i ≤ m` are independent, then
`D[X_[m] | Y_[m]] = ∑_{(y_i)_{1 ≤ i ≤ m}} P(Y_i=y_i ∀ i) D[(X_i | Y_i=y_i ∀ i)_{i=1}^m]`.

name: multiDist_chainRule
signature: {G H : Type*} [hG : MeasurableSpace G] [MeasurableSingletonClass G] [AddCommGroup G] [Finite G] [hH : MeasurableSpace H] [MeasurableSingletonClass H] [AddCommGroup H] [Fintype H] (π : G →+ H) {m : ℕ} {Ω : Type*} (hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω) {X : Fin m → Ω → G} (hmes : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (h_indep : iIndepFun X) : D[X; fun _ ↦ hΩ] = D[X | fun i ↦ π ∘ X i; fun _ ↦ hΩ] + D[fun i ↦ π ∘ X i; fun _ ↦ hΩ] + I[∑ i, X i : fun ω ↦ (fun i ↦ π (X i ω)) | π ∘ (∑ i, X i)]
docstring:
Let `π : G → H` be a homomorphism of abelian groups and let `X_[m]` be a tuple of jointly
independent `G`-valued random variables. Then `D[X_[m]]` is equal to
`D[X_[m] | π(X_[m])] + D[π(X_[m])] + I[∑ i, X_i : π(X_[m]) ; | ; π(∑ i, X_i)]`
where `π(X_[m]) := (π(X_1), ..., π(X_m))`.

name: cond_multiDist_chainRule
signature: {G H : Type*} [hG : MeasurableSpace G] [MeasurableSingletonClass G] [AddCommGroup G] [Finite G] [hH : MeasurableSpace H] [MeasurableSingletonClass H] [AddCommGroup H] [Fintype H] (π : G →+ H) {S : Type*} [Fintype S] [hS : MeasurableSpace S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] {m : ℕ} {Ω : Type*} [hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω] {X : Fin m → Ω → G} (hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) {Y : Fin m → Ω → S} (hY : ∀ i, Measurable (Y i)) (h_indep : iIndepFun (fun i ↦ ⟨X i, Y i⟩)) : D[X | Y; fun _ ↦ hΩ] = D[X | fun i ↦ ⟨π ∘ X i, Y i⟩; fun _ ↦ hΩ] + D[fun i ↦ π ∘ X i | Y; fun _ ↦ hΩ] + I[∑ i, X i : fun ω ↦ (fun i ↦ π (X i ω)) | ⟨π ∘ (∑ i, X i), fun ω ↦ (fun i ↦ Y i ω)⟩]
docstring:
Let `π : G → H` be a homomorphism of abelian groups. Let `I` be a finite index set and let
`X_[m]` be a tuple of `G`-valued random variables. Let `Y_[m]` be another tuple of random variables
(not necessarily `G`-valued). Suppose that the pairs `(X_i, Y_i)` are jointly independent of one
another (but `X_i` need not be independent of `Y_i`). Then
`D[X_[m] | Y_[m]] = D[X_[m] ,|, π(X_[m]), Y_[m]] + D[π(X_[m]) ,| , Y_[m]]`
`+ I[∑ i, X_i : π(X_[m]) ; | ; π(∑ i, X_i), Y_[m]]`.

name: iter_multiDist_chainRule
signature: {m : ℕ} {G : Fin (m + 1) → Type*} [hG : ∀ i, MeasurableSpace (G i)] [hGs : ∀ i, MeasurableSingletonClass (G i)] [∀ i, AddCommGroup (G i)] [hGcount : ∀ i, Fintype (G i)] {φ : ∀ i : Fin m, G (i.succ) →+ G i.castSucc} {π : ∀ d, G ⊤ →+ G d} (hcomp: ∀ i : Fin m, π i.castSucc = (φ i) ∘ (π i.succ)) {Ω : Type*} [hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω] {X : Fin m → Ω → G ⊤} (hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (h_indep : iIndepFun X) (n : Fin (m + 1)) : D[X | fun i ↦ (π 0) ∘ X i; fun _ ↦ hΩ] = D[X | fun i ↦ (π n) ∘ X i; fun _ ↦ hΩ] + ∑ d ∈ Finset.Iio n, (D[fun i ↦ (π (d+1)) ∘ X i | fun i ↦ (π d) ∘ X i; fun _ ↦ hΩ] + I[∑ i, X i : fun ω ↦ (fun i ↦ (π (d+1)) (X i ω)) | ⟨(π (d+1)) ∘ ∑ i, X i, fun ω ↦ (fun i ↦ (π d) (X i ω))⟩])
docstring:
Let `m` be a positive integer. Suppose one has a sequence
`G_m → G_{m - 1} → ... → G_1 → G_0 = {0}` of homomorphisms between abelian groups `G_0, ...,G_m`,
and for each `d=0, ...,m`, let `π_d : G_m → G_d` be the homomorphism from `G_m` to `G_d` arising
from this sequence by composition
(so for instance `π_m` is the identity homomorphism and `π_0` is the zero homomorphism).
Let `X_[m] = (X_1, ..., X_m)` be a jointly independent tuple of `G_m`-valued random variables.
Then `D[X_[m]] = ∑ d, D[π_d(X_[m]) ,| , π_(d-1)(X_[m])]`
` + ∑_{d=1}^{m - 1}, I[∑ i, X_i : π_d(X_[m]) | π_d(∑ i, X_i), π_(d-1})(X_[m])]`.

name: iter_multiDist_chainRule'
signature: {m : ℕ} (hm : m > 0) {G : Fin (m + 1) → Type*} [hG : ∀ i, MeasurableSpace (G i)] [hGs : ∀ i, MeasurableSingletonClass (G i)] [hGa : ∀ i, AddCommGroup (G i)] [hGcount : ∀ i, Fintype (G i)] {φ : ∀ i : Fin m, G (i.succ) →+ G i.castSucc} {π : ∀ d, G ⊤ →+ G d} (hπ0 : π 0 = 0) (hcomp : ∀ i : Fin m, π i.castSucc = φ i ∘ π i.succ) {Ω : Type*} [hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω] {X : Fin m → Ω → G ⊤} (hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (h_indep : iIndepFun X) : D[X; fun _ ↦ hΩ] ≥ ∑ d : Fin m, D[fun i ↦ π d.succ ∘ X i | fun i ↦ π d.castSucc ∘ X i; fun _ ↦ hΩ] + I[∑ i : Fin m, X i : fun ω i ↦ π 1 (X i ω)| π 1 ∘ ∑ i : Fin m, X i]
docstring:
Under the preceding hypotheses,
`D[X_[m]] ≥ ∑ d, D[π_d(X_[m])| π_(d-1})(X_[m])] + I[∑ i, X_i : π_1(X_[m]) | π_1(∑ i, X_i)]`.

name: multiDist_of_hom'
signature: {G G' : Type*} [MeasurableSpace G] [MeasurableSingletonClass G] [AddCommGroup G] [Finite G] [MeasurableSpace G'] [MeasurableSingletonClass G'] [AddCommGroup G'] [Finite G'] {ι : G →+ G'} (hι : Function.Injective ⇑ι) {m : ℕ} {Ω : Fin m → Type*} (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) [∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : Measure (Ω i))] {X : ∀ i, Ω i → G} (hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (a : Fin m → G') : D[fun i ω ↦ ι (X i ω) + a i; hΩ] = D[X; hΩ]

name: multiDist_of_hom
signature: {G G' : Type*} [MeasurableSpace G] [MeasurableSingletonClass G] [AddCommGroup G] [Finite G] [MeasurableSpace G'] [MeasurableSingletonClass G'] [AddCommGroup G'] [Finite G'] {ι : G →+ G'} (hι : Function.Injective ⇑ι) {Ω : Type*} (hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : Measure Ω)] {m : ℕ} {X : Fin m → Ω → G} (hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) : D[fun i ω ↦ ι (X i ω); fun _ ↦ hΩ] = D[X; fun _ ↦ hΩ]

name: multiDist_congr
signature: {G : Type*} [MeasurableSpace G] [AddCommGroup G] {m : ℕ} {Ω : Fin m → Type*} (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) {X X' : ∀ i, Ω i → G} (hae : ∀ i, X i =ᵐ[ℙ] X' i) : D[X; hΩ] = D[X'; hΩ]

name: condMultiDist_of_hom
signature: {G G' S : Type*} [Fintype S] [MeasurableSpace G] [MeasurableSingletonClass G] [AddCommGroup G] [Finite G] [MeasurableSpace G'] [MeasurableSingletonClass G'] [AddCommGroup G'] [Finite G'] [MeasurableSpace S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] {ι : G →+ G'} (hι : Function.Injective ⇑ι) {Ω : Type*} (hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : Measure Ω)] {m : ℕ} {X : Fin m → Ω → G} (hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) {Y : Fin m → Ω → S} (hY : ∀ i, Measurable (Y i)) (a : Fin m → S → G') : D[fun i ω ↦ ι (X i ω) + a i (Y i ω) | Y; fun _ ↦ hΩ] = D[X | Y; fun _ ↦ hΩ]

name: cond_entropy_indep
signature: {Ω : Type*} [hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω] {S T U : Type*} {X : Ω → S} {Y : Ω → T} {Z : Ω → U} [MeasurableSpace S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] [Finite S] [MeasurableSpace T] [MeasurableSingletonClass T] [Finite T] [MeasurableSpace U] [MeasurableSingletonClass U] [Finite U] (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (hZ : Measurable Z) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure hΩ.volume] (hindep : IndepFun (⟨X, Y⟩) Z) : H[X | ⟨Y, Z⟩] = H[X | Y]

name: cor_multiDist_chainRule
signature: [Fintype G] {m : ℕ} {Ω : Type*} (hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω) (X : Fin (m + 1) × Fin (m + 1) → Ω → G) (hmes : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (h_indep : iIndepFun X) [IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : Measure Ω)]: I[fun ω ↦ (fun j ↦ ∑ i, X ⟨i, j⟩ ω) : fun ω ↦ (fun i ↦ ∑ j, X ⟨i, j⟩ ω) | ∑ p, X p] ≤ ∑ j : Fin m, (D[fun i ↦ X ⟨i, j.castSucc⟩; fun _ ↦ hΩ] - D[fun i ↦ X ⟨i, j.castSucc⟩ | fun i ↦ ∑ k ∈ Finset.Ici j.castSucc, X ⟨i, k⟩; fun _ ↦ hΩ]) + D[fun i ↦ X ⟨i, ⊤⟩; fun _ ↦ hΩ] - D[fun i ↦ ∑ j, X ⟨i, j⟩; fun _ ↦ hΩ]
docstring:
Let `G` be an abelian group and let `m ≥ 2`. Suppose that `X_{i,j}`, `1 ≤ i, j ≤ m`, are
independent `G`-valued random variables. Then
`I[(∑ i, X_{i,j})_{j=1}^m : (∑ j, X_{i,j})_{i=1}^m | ∑ i j, X_{i,j}]`
is less than
`∑_{j=1}^{m - 1} (D[(X_{i, j})_{i=1}^m]`
`- D[(X_{i, j})_{i = 1}^m | (X_{i,j} + ... + X_{i,m})_{i=1}^m])`
`+ D[(X_{i,m})_{i=1}^m] - D[(∑ j, X_{i,j})_{i=1}^m],`
where all the multidistances here involve the indexing set `{1, ..., m}`.

name: measureReal_preimage_sub_zero
signature: [DecidableEq G] (Uunif : IsUniform A U) (Umeas : Measurable U) (Vunif : IsUniform B V) (Vmeas : Measurable V) (h_indep : IndepFun U V) : (ℙ : Measure Ω).real ((U - V) ⁻¹' {0}) = (A ∩ B).card / (A.card * B.card)
docstring:
Given two independent random variables `U` and `V` uniformly distributed respectively on `A`
and `B`, then `U = V` with probability `#(A ∩ B) / #A ⬝ #B`.

name: measureReal_preimage_sub
signature: [DecidableEq G] (Uunif : IsUniform A U) (Umeas : Measurable U) (Vunif : IsUniform B V) (Vmeas : Measurable V) (h_indep : IndepFun U V) (x : G) : (ℙ : Measure Ω).real ((U - V) ⁻¹' {x}) = (A ∩ (B + {x})).card / (A.card * B.card)
docstring:
Given two independent random variables `U` and `V` uniformly distributed respectively on `A`
and `B`, then `U = V + x` with probability `# (A ∩ (B + x)) / #A ⬝ #B`.

name: PFR_conjecture_pos_aux
signature: {G : Type*} [AddCommGroup G] {A : Set G} (hA : A.Finite) {K : ℝ} (hA₀ : A.Nonempty) (hAK : (A - A).ncard ≤ K * A.ncard) : (0 : ℝ) < A.ncard ∧ (0 : ℝ) < (A - A).ncard ∧ 0 < K
docstring: Record positivity results that are useful in the proof of PFR.

name: PFR_conjecture_pos_aux'
signature: {G : Type*} [AddCommGroup G] {A : Set G} (hA : A.Finite) {K : ℝ} (hA₀ : A.Nonempty) (hAK : (A + A).ncard ≤ K * A.ncard) : (0 : ℝ) < A.ncard ∧ (0 : ℝ) < (A + A).ncard ∧ 0 < K

name: rdist_le_of_isUniform_of_card_add_le
signature: [A_fin : Finite A] [MeasurableSpace G] [MeasurableSingletonClass G] (hA₀ : A.Nonempty) (hA : (A - A).ncard ≤ K * A.ncard) {Ω : Type*} [MeasureSpace Ω] [IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : Measure Ω)] {U₀ : Ω → G} (U₀unif : IsUniform A U₀) (U₀meas : Measurable U₀) : d[U₀ # U₀] ≤ log K
docstring:
A uniform distribution on a set with doubling constant `K` has self Rusza distance
at most `log K`.

name: sumset_eq_sub
signature: {G : Type*} [AddCommGroup G] [Module (ZMod 2) G] (A : Set G) : A + A = A - A

name: PFR_conjecture_aux
signature: (hA₀ : A.Nonempty) (hA : (A + A).ncard ≤ K * A.ncard) : ∃ (H : Submodule (ZMod 2) G) (c : Set G), Nat.card c ≤ K ^ (13/2 : ℝ) * A.ncard ^ (1/2 : ℝ) * (H : Set G).ncard ^ (-1/2 : ℝ) ∧ (H : Set G).ncard ≤ K ^ 11 * A.ncard ∧ A.ncard ≤ K ^ 11 * (H : Set G).ncard ∧ A ⊆ c + H
docstring:
Auxiliary statement towards the polynomial Freiman-Ruzsa (PFR) conjecture: if `A` is a subset of
an elementary abelian 2-group of doubling constant at most $K$, then there exists a subgroup `H`
such that `A` can be covered by at most `K^(13/2) |A|^(1/2) / |H|^(1/2)` cosets of `H`, and `H` has
the same cardinality as `A` up to a multiplicative factor `K^11`.

name: PFR_conjecture
signature: (hA₀ : A.Nonempty) (hA : (A + A).ncard ≤ K * A.ncard) : ∃ (H : Submodule (ZMod 2) G) (c : Set G), Nat.card c < 2 * K ^ 12 ∧ (H : Set G).ncard ≤ A.ncard ∧ A ⊆ c + H
docstring:
The polynomial Freiman-Ruzsa (PFR) conjecture: if `A` is a subset of an elementary abelian
2-group of doubling constant at most `K`, then `A` can be covered by at most `2 * K ^ 12` cosets of
a subgroup of cardinality at most `|A|`.

name: PFR_conjecture'
signature: {G : Type*} [AddCommGroup G] [Module (ZMod 2) G] {A : Set G} {K : ℝ} (hA₀ : A.Nonempty) (Afin : A.Finite) (hA : (A + A).ncard ≤ K * A.ncard) : ∃ (H : Submodule (ZMod 2) G) (c : Set G), c.Finite ∧ (H : Set G).Finite ∧ Nat.card c < 2 * K ^ 12 ∧ (H : Set G).ncard ≤ A.ncard ∧ A ⊆ c + H
docstring:
Corollary of `PFR_conjecture` in which the ambient group is not required to be finite (but) then
`H` and `c` are finite.

name: Q_eq
signature: (j : Fin p.m) : Q j = ∑ i, Y (i, j)

name: indep_yj
signature: (j : Fin p.m) : iIndepFun (fun i ↦ Y (i, j))

name: torsion_mul_eq
signature: {i j : ℤ} (x : G) (h : i ≡ j [ZMOD p.m]) : i • x = j • x

name: Q_ident
signature: (j j' : Fin p.m) : IdentDistrib (Q j) (Q j') ℙ ℙ

signature: (j j' : Fin p.m) : d[Q j # -(Q j')] ≤ 2 * k

signature: (j : Fin p.m) : H[Q j] = k + (↑p.m)⁻¹ * ∑ i, H[X i]

signature: {j j' : Fin p.m} (h : j ≠ j') : IndepFun (Q j) (-Q j') ℙ

name: entropy_of_W_le
docstring: We have $\bbH[W] \leq (2m-1)k + \frac1m \sum_{i=1}^m \bbH[X_i]$.

name: Z2_eq
signature: : Z2 = ∑ j ∈ Finset.univ.erase ⟨0, by linarith [p.hm]⟩, j.val • Q j

name: entropy_of_Z_two_le
docstring: We have $\bbH[Z_2] \leq (8m^2-16m+1) k + \frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=1}^m \bbH[X_i]$.

name: mutual_of_W_Z_two_le
docstring: We have $\bbI[W : Z_2] \leq 2(m-1) k$.

name: sum_of_conditional_distance_le
docstring: We have $\sum_{i=1}^m d[X_i;Z_2|W] \leq 4(m^3-m^2) k$.

name: pigeonhole
signature: {G : Type*} [MeasureSpace G] [IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : Measure G)] [Finite G] [MeasurableSingletonClass G] (f : G → ℝ) : ∃ x, f x ≤ ∫ (z : G), f z ∧ ℙ {x} ≠ 0

name: dist_of_U_add_le
signature: {G : Type*} [MeasurableFinGroup G] {Ω : Type u} [hΩ : MeasureSpace Ω] [IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : Measure Ω)] {T₁ T₂ T₃ : Ω → G} (hsum : T₁ + T₂ + T₃ = 0) (hmes₁ : Measurable T₁) (hmes₂ : Measurable T₂) (hmes₃ : Measurable T₃) {n : ℕ} {Ω' : Fin n → Type*} (hΩ' : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω' i)) [∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ' i).volume] {Y : ∀ i, (Ω' i) → G} (hY : ∀ i, Measurable (Y i)) {α : ℝ} (hα : α > 0) : ∃ (Ω'' : Type u) (hΩ'': MeasureSpace Ω'') (U: Ω'' → G), IsProbabilityMeasure hΩ''.volume ∧ Measurable U ∧ d[U # U] + α * ∑ i, d[Y i # U] ≤ (2 + α * n / 2) * (I[T₁ : T₂] + I[T₁ : T₃] + I[T₂ : T₃]) + α * ∑ i, d[Y i # T₂]
docstring:
Let $G$ be an abelian group, let $(T_1,T_2,T_3)$ be a $G^3$-valued random variable such that
$T_1+T_2+T_3=0$ holds identically, and write
  \[
    \delta := \bbI[T_1 : T_2] + \bbI[T_1 : T_3] + \bbI[T_2 : T_3].
  \]
  Let $Y_1,\dots,Y_n$ be some further $G$-valued random variables and let $\alpha>0$ be a constant.
  Then there exists a random variable $U$ such that
$$  d[U;U] + \alpha \sum_{i=1}^n d[Y_i;U] \leq
  \Bigl(2 + \frac{\alpha n}{2} \Bigr) \delta + \alpha \sum_{i=1}^n d[Y_i;T_2].$$

name: k_eq_zero
docstring: We have $k = 0$.

name: dist_of_X_U_H_le
signature: {G : Type u} [AddCommGroup G] [Finite G] [MeasurableSpace G] [MeasurableSingletonClass G] {m : ℕ} (hm : m ≥ 2) (htorsion : ∀ x:G, m • x = 0) {Ω : Type u} [MeasureSpace Ω] [IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ:Measure Ω)] {X: Ω → G} (hX: Measurable X) : ∃ H : AddSubgroup G, ∃ Ω' : Type u, ∃ mΩ : MeasureSpace Ω', IsProbabilityMeasure mΩ.volume ∧ ∃ U : Ω' → G, IsUniform H U ∧ Measurable U ∧ d[X # U] ≤ 64 * m^3 * d[X # X]
docstring:
Suppose that $G$ is a finite abelian group of torsion $m$. Suppose that $X$ is a $G$-valued
random variable. Then there exists a subgroup $H \leq G$ such that
\[ d[X;U_H] \leq 64 m^3 d[X;X].\].

name: rdist_le_of_isUniform_of_card_add_le'
signature: {G : Type*} [AddCommGroup G] {A : Set G} {K : ℝ} [Countable G] [A_fin : Finite A] [MeasurableSpace G] [MeasurableSingletonClass G] (h₀A : A.Nonempty) (hA : (A + A).ncard ≤ K * A.ncard) {Ω : Type*} [MeasureSpace Ω] [IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : Measure Ω)] {U₀ : Ω → G} (U₀unif : IsUniform A U₀) (U₀meas : Measurable U₀) : d[U₀ # -U₀] ≤ log K
docstring:
A uniform distribution on a set with doubling constant `K` has self Rusza distance
at most `log K`.

name: torsion_PFR_conjecture_aux
signature: {G : Type*} [AddCommGroup G] [Finite G] {m : ℕ} (hm : m ≥ 2) (htorsion : ∀ x:G, m • x = 0) {A : Set G} [A_fin: Finite A] {K : ℝ} (h₀A : A.Nonempty) (hA : Nat.card (A + A) ≤ K * A.ncard) : ∃ (H : AddSubgroup G) (c : Set G), c.ncard ≤ K ^ (128 * m^3 + 1) * A.ncard ^ (1/2:ℝ) * (H : Set G).ncard ^ (-1/2 : ℝ) ∧ (H : Set G).ncard ≤ K ^ (256 * m^3) * A.ncard ∧ A.ncard ≤ K ^ (256 * m^3) * (H : Set G).ncard ∧ A ⊆ c + H
docstring:
Suppose that $G$ is a finite abelian group of torsion $m$. If $A \subset G$ is non-empty and
$|A+A| \leq K|A|$, then $A$ can be covered by at most $K ^
{(64m^3+2)/2}|A|^{1/2}/|H|^{1/2}$ translates of a subspace $H$ of $G$ with
$|H|/|A| \in [K^{-64m^3}, K^{64m^3}]$.

name: torsion_exists_subgroup_subset_card_le
signature: {G : Type*} {m : ℕ} (hm : m ≥ 2) [AddCommGroup G] [Finite G] (htorsion : ∀ x:G, m • x = 0) {k : ℕ} (H : AddSubgroup G) (hk : k ≤ (H : Set G).ncard) (h'k : k ≠ 0) : ∃ (K : AddSubgroup G), (K : Set G).ncard ≤ k ∧ k < m * (K : Set G).ncard ∧ K ≤ H
docstring:
Every subgroup `H` of a finite `m`-torsion abelian group `G` contains a subgroup `H'` of order
between `k` and `mk`, if `0 < k < |H|`.

name: torsion_PFR
signature: {G : Type*} [AddCommGroup G] [Finite G] {m : ℕ} (hm : m ≥ 2) (htorsion : ∀ x:G, m • x = 0) {A : Set G} [Finite A] {K : ℝ} (h₀A : A.Nonempty) (hA : Nat.card (A + A) ≤ K * A.ncard) : ∃ (H : AddSubgroup G) (c : Set G), Nat.card c < m * K ^ (256*m^3+1) ∧ (H : Set G).ncard ≤ A.ncard ∧ A ⊆ c + H
docstring:
Suppose that $G$ is a finite abelian group of torsion $m$.
If $A \subset G$ is non-empty and $|A+A| \leq K|A|$, then $A$ can be covered by most $mK^{64m^3+1}$
translates of a subspace $H$ of $G$ with $|H| \leq |A|$.

name: MeasurableFinGroup
signature: (G : Type*) extends AddCommGroup G, Fintype G, MeasurableSpace G, MeasurableSingletonClass G /-- A structure that packages all the fixed information in the main argument. See https://leanprover.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/270676-lean4/topic/Problem.20when.20instances.20are.20inside.20a.20structure for more discussion of the design choices here. -/ structure multiRefPackage (G Ω₀ : Type u) [MeasurableFinGroup G] [MeasureSpace Ω₀]
docstring: We will frequently be working with finite additive groups with the discrete sigma-algebra.

name: multiTau
signature: {G Ω₀ : Type u} [MeasurableFinGroup G] [MeasureSpace Ω₀] (p : multiRefPackage G Ω₀) (Ω : Fin p.m → Type*) (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) (X : ∀ i, Ω i → G) : ℝ
docstring:
If $(X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}$ is a tuple, we define its $\tau$-functional
$$ \tau[ (X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}] := D[(X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}] + \eta \sum_{i=1}^m d[X_i; X^0].$$

name: multiTau_of_ident
signature: {G Ω₀ : Type u} [MeasurableFinGroup G] [MeasureSpace Ω₀] (p : multiRefPackage G Ω₀) {Ω₁ Ω₂ : Fin p.m → Type*} (hΩ₁ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω₁ i)) (hΩ₂ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω₂ i)) {X₁ : ∀ i, Ω₁ i → G} {X₂ : ∀ i, Ω₂ i → G} (h_ident : ∀ i, IdentDistrib (X₁ i) (X₂ i)) : multiTau p Ω₁ hΩ₁ X₁ = multiTau p Ω₂ hΩ₂ X₂

name: multiTauMinimizes
signature: {G Ω₀ : Type u} [MeasurableFinGroup G] [MeasureSpace Ω₀] (p : multiRefPackage G Ω₀) (Ω : Fin p.m → Type u) (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) (X : ∀ i, Ω i → G) : Prop
docstring:
A $\tau$-minimizer is a tuple $(X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}$ that minimizes the $\tau$-functional
among all tuples of $G$-valued random variables.

name: multiTauMinimizes_of_ident
signature: {G Ω₀ : Type u} [MeasurableFinGroup G] [MeasureSpace Ω₀] (p : multiRefPackage G Ω₀) {Ω₁ Ω₂ : Fin p.m → Type u} (hΩ₁ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω₁ i)) (hΩ₂ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω₂ i)) {X₁ : ∀ i, Ω₁ i → G} {X₂ : ∀ i, Ω₂ i → G} (h_ident : ∀ i, IdentDistrib (X₁ i) (X₂ i)) (hmin : multiTauMinimizes p Ω₁ hΩ₁ X₁) : multiTauMinimizes p Ω₂ hΩ₂ X₂

name: multiTau_continuous
signature: {G Ω₀ : Type u} [MeasurableFinGroup G] [TopologicalSpace G] [DiscreteTopology G] [BorelSpace G] [MeasureSpace Ω₀] (p : multiRefPackage G Ω₀) : Continuous (fun (μ : Fin p.m → ProbabilityMeasure G) ↦ multiTau p (fun _ ↦ G) (fun i ↦ ⟨μ i⟩) (fun _ ↦ id))
docstring: If $G$ is finite, then $\tau$ is continuous.

name: multiTau_min_exists_measure
signature: {G Ω₀ : Type u} [MeasurableFinGroup G] [MeasureSpace Ω₀] (p : multiRefPackage G Ω₀) : ∃ (μ : Fin p.m → Measure G), (∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (μ i)) ∧ ∀ (ν : Fin p.m → Measure G), (∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (ν i)) → multiTau p (fun _ ↦ G) (fun i ↦ ⟨μ i⟩) (fun _ ↦ id) ≤ multiTau p (fun _ ↦ G) (fun i ↦ ⟨ν i⟩) (fun _ ↦ id)
docstring: If $G$ is finite, then a $\tau$-minimizer exists.

name: multiTau_min_exists
signature: {G Ω₀ : Type u} [MeasurableFinGroup G] [MeasureSpace Ω₀] (p : multiRefPackage G Ω₀) : ∃ (Ω : Fin p.m → Type u) (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) (X : ∀ i, Ω i → G), (∀ i, Measurable (X i)) ∧ (∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ i).volume) ∧ multiTauMinimizes p Ω hΩ X
docstring: If $G$ is finite, then a $\tau$-minimizer exists.

name: multiTau_min_sum_le
signature: {G Ω₀ : Type u} [hG : MeasurableFinGroup G] [hΩ₀ : MeasureSpace Ω₀] (p : multiRefPackage G Ω₀) (Ω : Fin p.m → Type u) (hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)) (hprobΩ : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (ℙ : Measure (Ω i))) (X : ∀ i, Ω i → G) (hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (h_min : multiTauMinimizes p Ω hΩ X) : ∑ i, d[X i # p.X₀] ≤ 2 * p.m * p.η⁻¹ * d[p.X₀ # p.X₀]
docstring:
If $(X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}$ is a $\tau$-minimizer,
then $\sum_{i=1}^m d[X_i; X^0] \leq \frac{2m}{\eta} d[X^0; X^0]$.

name: sub_multiDistance_le
signature: {G Ω₀ : Type u} [MeasurableFinGroup G] [hΩ₀ : MeasureSpace Ω₀] {p : multiRefPackage G Ω₀} {Ω : Fin p.m → Type u} {hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)} (hΩprob : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ i).volume) {X : ∀ i, Ω i → G} (hmeasX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (h_min : multiTauMinimizes p Ω hΩ X) {Ω' : Fin p.m → Type u} {hΩ' : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω' i)} (hΩprob' : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ' i).volume) {X' : ∀ i, Ω' i → G} (hmeasX' : ∀ i, Measurable (X' i)) : D[X; hΩ] - D[X'; hΩ'] ≤ p.η * ∑ i, d[X i ; (hΩ i).volume # X' i; (hΩ' i).volume ]
docstring:
If $(X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}$ is a $\tau$-minimizer, and $k := D[(X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}]$,
then for any other tuple $(X'_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}$, one has
  $$ k - D[(X'_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}] \leq \eta \sum_{i=1}^m d[X_i; X'_i].$$

name: sub_multiDistance_le'
signature: {G Ω₀ : Type u} [MeasurableFinGroup G] [MeasureSpace Ω₀] {p : multiRefPackage G Ω₀} {Ω : Fin p.m → Type u} {hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)} (hΩprob : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ i).volume) {X : ∀ i, Ω i → G} (hmeasX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (h_min : multiTauMinimizes p Ω hΩ X) {Ω' : Fin p.m → Type u} {hΩ' : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω' i)} (hΩ'prob : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ' i).volume) {X' : ∀ i, Ω' i → G} (hmeasX' : ∀ i, Measurable (X' i)) (φ : Equiv.Perm (Fin p.m)) : D[X; hΩ] - D[X'; hΩ'] ≤ p.η * ∑ i, d[X (φ i) ; (hΩ (φ i)).volume # X' i; (hΩ' i).volume ]

name: sub_condMultiDistance_le
signature: {G Ω₀ : Type u} [MeasurableFinGroup G] [MeasureSpace Ω₀] {p : multiRefPackage G Ω₀} {Ω : Fin p.m → Type u} {hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)} (hΩprob : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ i).volume) {X : ∀ i, Ω i → G} (hmeasX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (h_min : multiTauMinimizes p Ω hΩ X) {Ω' : Fin p.m → Type u} {hΩ' : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω' i)} (hΩ'prob : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ' i).volume) {X' : ∀ i, Ω' i → G} (hmeasX' : ∀ i, Measurable (X' i)) {S : Type u} [Fintype S] [MeasurableSpace S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] {Y : ∀ i, Ω' i → S} (hY : ∀ i, Measurable (Y i)) : D[X; hΩ] - D[X'|Y; hΩ'] ≤ p.η * ∑ i, d[X i ; (hΩ i).volume # X' i | Y i; (hΩ' i).volume]
docstring:
If $(X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}$ is a $\tau$-minimizer, and $k := D[(X_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}]$,
then for any other tuples $(X'_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}$ and $(Y_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}$ with the $X'_i$
G$-valued, one has
$$ k - D[(X'_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m} | (Y_i)_{1 \leq i \leq m}]
  \leq \eta \sum_{i=1}^m d[X_i; X'_i|Y_i].$$

name: sub_condMultiDistance_le'
signature: {G Ω₀ : Type u} [MeasurableFinGroup G] [MeasureSpace Ω₀] {p : multiRefPackage G Ω₀} {Ω : Fin p.m → Type u} {hΩ : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω i)} (hΩprob : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ i).volume) {X : ∀ i, Ω i → G} (hmeasX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (h_min : multiTauMinimizes p Ω hΩ X) {Ω' : Fin p.m → Type u} {hΩ' : ∀ i, MeasureSpace (Ω' i)} (hΩ'prob : ∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (hΩ' i).volume) {X' : ∀ i, Ω' i → G} (hmeasX' : ∀ i, Measurable (X' i)) {S : Type u} [Fintype S] [MeasurableSpace S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] {Y : ∀ i, Ω' i → S} (hY : ∀ i, Measurable (Y i)) (φ : Equiv.Perm (Fin p.m)) : D[X; hΩ] - D[X'|Y; hΩ'] ≤ p.η * ∑ i, d[X (φ i) ; (hΩ (φ i)).volume # X' i | Y i; (hΩ' i).volume ]

The monad for `RingNF` contains, in addition to the `AtomM` state,

name: ExBase
signature: : (e : Q(ℝ)) → Type /-- A coefficient `value`, which must not be `0`. `e` is a raw rat cast. If `value` is not an integer, then `hyp` should be a proof of `(value.den : α) ≠ 0`. -/ | atom (id : ℕ) (x : Q(ℝ)) : ExBase x /-- A product `x ^ e * b` is a monomial if `b` is a monomial. Here `x` is an `ExBase` and `e` is an `ExBase` representing a monomial expression in `ℕ` (it is a monomial instead of a polynomial because we eagerly normalize `x ^ (a + b) = x ^ a * x ^ b`.) -/ | pow (id : ℕ) (x : Q(ℝ)) (h : Q(0 < $x)) (e : Q(ℝ)) : ExBase q($x ^ $e) def ExBase.id {e : Q(ℝ)} : ExBase e → ℕ | .atom id .. | .pow id .. => id /-- A monomial, which is a product of powers of `ExBase` expressions, terminated by a (nonzero) constant coefficient. -/ inductive ExProd : (e : Q(ℝ)) → Type /-- A coefficient `value`, which must not be `0`. `e` is a raw rat cast. If `value` is not an integer, then `hyp` should be a proof of `(value.den : α) ≠ 0`. -/ | one : ExProd q(1) /-- A product `x ^ e * b` is a monomial if `b` is a monomial. Here `x` is an `ExBase` and `e` is an `ExProd` representing a monomial expression in `ℕ` (it is a monomial instead of a polynomial because we eagerly normalize `x ^ (a + b) = x ^ a * x ^ b`.) -/ | mul {x b : Q(ℝ)} : ExBase x → ExProd b → ExProd q($x * $b) instance : Inhabited (Σ e, ExBase e)
docstring:
A monomial, which is a product of powers of `ExBase` expressions,
terminated by a (nonzero) constant coefficient.

signature: {a₂ b₂ c x : ℝ} (ea eb : ℝ) (h : 0 < x) (_ : a₂ * b₂ = c) : (x ^ ea * a₂ : ℝ) * (x ^ eb * b₂) = x ^ (ea + eb) * c

name: evalMul
signature: {a b : Q(ℝ)} (va : ExProd a) (vb : ExProd b) : Result ExProd q($a * $b)
docstring:
Multiplies two monomials `va, vb` together to get a normalized result monomial.

* `x * y = (x * y)` (for `x`, `y` coefficients)
* `x * (b₁ * b₂) = b₁ * (b₂ * x)` (for `x` coefficient)
* `(a₁ * a₂) * y = a₁ * (a₂ * y)` (for `y` coefficient)
* `(x ^ ea * a₂) * (x ^ eb * b₂) = x ^ (ea + eb) * (a₂ * b₂)`
    (if `ea` and `eb` are identical except coefficient)
* `(a₁ * a₂) * (b₁ * b₂) = a₁ * (a₂ * (b₁ * b₂))` (if `a₁.lt b₁`)
* `(a₁ * a₂) * (b₁ * b₂) = b₁ * ((a₁ * a₂) * b₂)` (if not `a₁.lt b₁`)

name: sum_mul_log_div_leq
signature: {a b : ι → ℝ} (ha : ∀ i ∈ s, 0 ≤ a i) (hb : ∀ i ∈ s, 0 ≤ b i) (habs : ∀ i ∈ s, b i = 0 → a i = 0) : (∑ i ∈ s, a i) * log ((∑ i ∈ s, a i) / (∑ i ∈ s, b i)) ≤ ∑ i ∈ s, a i * log (a i / b i)
docstring:
If $S$ is a finite set, and $a_s$, $b_s$ are non-negative for $s\in S$, then
  $$\sum_{s\in S} a_s \log\frac{a_s}{b_s}\ge
    \left(\sum_{s\in S}a_s\right)\log\frac{\sum_{s\in S} a_s}{\sum_{s\in S} b_s}$$.
  We require additionally that, if $b_s=0$, then $s_s=0$ as otherwise the right hand side should
  morally be infinite, which it can't be in the formalization using real numbers.

name: sum_mul_log_div_eq_iff_aux
signature: {a b : ι → ℝ} (ha : ∀ i ∈ s, 0 ≤ a i) (hb : ∀ i ∈ s, 0 < b i) (heq : ∑ i ∈ s, a i * log (a i / b i) = (∑ i ∈ s, a i) * log ((∑ i ∈ s, a i) / ∑ i ∈ s, b i)) : ∃ r, ∀ i ∈ s, a i = r * (b i)
docstring:
If equality holds in the previous bound, then $a_s=r\cdot b_s$ for every $s\in S$, for some
constant $r\in \mathbb{R}$. Auxiliary version assuming the `b i` are positive.

name: sum_mul_log_div_eq_iff
signature: {a b : ι → ℝ} (ha : ∀ i ∈ s, 0 ≤ a i) (hb : ∀ i ∈ s, 0 ≤ b i) (habs : ∀ i ∈ s, b i = 0 → a i = 0) (heq : ∑ i ∈ s, a i * log (a i / b i) = (∑ i ∈ s, a i) * log ((∑ i ∈ s, a i) / (∑ i ∈ s, b i))) : ∃ r, ∀ i ∈ s, a i = r * (b i)
docstring:
If equality holds in the previous bound, then $a_s=r\cdot b_s$ for every $s\in S$, for some
constant $r\in \mathbb{R}$.

signature: {I : Type u} [Finite I] {α : I → Type u'} [mS : ∀ i : I, MeasurableSpace (α i)] {Ω : I → Type v} [mΩ : ∀ i : I, MeasurableSpace (Ω i)] (X : ∀ i : I, Ω i → α i) (hX : ∀ i : I, Measurable (X i)) (μ : ∀ i : I, Measure (Ω i)) [∀ i, IsProbabilityMeasure (μ i)] : ∃ (A : Type (max u v)) (_ : MeasurableSpace A) (μA : Measure A) (X' : ∀ i, A → α i), IsProbabilityMeasure μA ∧ iIndepFun X' μA ∧ ∀ i : I, Measurable (X' i) ∧ IdentDistrib (X' i) (X i) μA (μ i)

signature: {f : ∀ i, Ω → β i} {J : Type*} [Finite J] (S : J → Finset ι) (h_disjoint : Set.PairwiseDisjoint Set.univ S) (hf_Indep : iIndepFun f μ) (hf_meas : ∀ i, Measurable (f i)) : iIndepFun (fun (j : J) ↦ fun a (i : S j) ↦ f i a) μ

measurable space `γ j`, then the family of random variables formed by `φ j (f i)_{i ∈ S j}` and

name: AEFiniteKernelSupport

docstring:
A kernel `κ` has almost everywhere finite support wrt a measure `μ` if, for almost every
point `t`, then `κ t` has finite support. Note that we don't require any uniformity wrt `t`.

name: IsUniform
signature: (H : Set S) (X : Ω → S) (μ : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop
docstring:
The assertion that the law of $X$ is the uniform probability measure on a finite set $H$.
While in applications $H$ will be non-empty finite set, $X$ measurable, and and $μ$ a probability
measure, it could be technically convenient to have a definition that works even without these
hypotheses. (For instance, `isUniform` would be well-defined, but false, for infinite `H`).

This should probably be refactored, requiring instead that `μ.map X = uniformOn H`.

docstring: Uniform distributions exist.

name: κ
signature: : Fin 3 → Type | 0 | 1 => Fin 1 | 2 => Fin 2 private def κ_equiv : (Σ i, κ i) ≃ Fin 4

name: fintype_kappa
signature: : ∀ (i : Fin 3), Fintype (κ i) | 0 | 1 | 2 => inferInstanceAs (Fintype (Fin _)) attribute [local instance] fintype_kappa in /-- If `(Z₁, Z₂, Z₃, Z₄)` are independent, so are `(Z₁, Z₂, φ Z₃ Z₄)` for any measurable `φ`. -/ lemma apply_two_last (hZ₁ : Measurable Z₁) (hZ₂ : Measurable Z₂) (hZ₃ : Measurable Z₃) (hZ₄ : Measurable Z₄) {phi : G → G → G} (hphi : Measurable phi.uncurry) : iIndepFun ![Z₁, Z₂, (fun ω ↦ phi (Z₃ ω) (Z₄ ω))]

name: identDistrib_cond
signature: [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] (hi : IndepFun A B μ) {s : Set β} (hs : MeasurableSet s) (hA : Measurable A) (hB : Measurable B) (h : μ (B ⁻¹' s) ≠ 0) : IdentDistrib A A μ (μ[|B ⁻¹' s])
docstring:
If `A` is independent from `B`, then conditioning on an event given by `B` does not change
the distribution of `A`.

name: cond_left
signature: (hi : IndepFun A B μ) {s : Set α} (hs : MeasurableSet s) (hA : Measurable A) : IndepFun A B (μ[| A⁻¹' s])
docstring:
If `A` is independent of `B`, then they remain independent when conditioning on an event
of the form `A ∈ s` of positive probability.

name: cond_right
signature: (h : IndepFun A B μ) {t : Set β} (ht : MeasurableSet t) (hB : Measurable B) : IndepFun A B (μ[| B⁻¹' t])
docstring:
If `A` is independent of `B`, then they remain independent when conditioning on an event
of the form `B ∈ t` of positive probability.

name: cond
signature: (hi : IndepFun A B μ) {s : Set α} {t : Set β} (hs : MeasurableSet s) (ht : MeasurableSet t) (hA : Measurable A) (hB : Measurable B) : IndepFun A B (μ[| A ⁻¹' s ∩ B ⁻¹' t])
docstring:
If `A` is independent of `B`, then they remain independent when conditioning on an event
of the form `A ∈ s ∩ B ∈ t` of positive probability.

name: CondIndepFun
signature: (f : Ω → α) (g : Ω → β) (h : Ω → γ) (μ : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop
docstring: The assertion that `f` and `g` are conditionally independent relative to `h`.

name: condIndepFun_iff
signature: : CondIndepFun f g h μ ↔ ∀ᵐ z ∂ (μ.map h), IndepFun f g (μ[|h ← z])

name: comp_right
signature: {i : Ω' → Ω} (hi : MeasurableEmbedding i) (hi' : ∀ᵐ a ∂μ, a ∈ range i) (hf : Measurable f) (hg : Measurable g) (hh : Measurable h) (hfg : CondIndepFun f g h μ) : CondIndepFun (f ∘ i) (g ∘ i) (h ∘ i) (μ.comap i)
docstring:
Composing independent functions with a measurable embedding of conull range gives independent
functions.

name: condIndep_copies
signature: (X : Ω → α) (Y : Ω → β) (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) [finY : FiniteRange Y] (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] : ∃ (Ω' : Type u) (_ : MeasurableSpace Ω') (X₁ X₂ : Ω' → α) (Y' : Ω' → β) (ν : Measure Ω'), IsProbabilityMeasure ν ∧ Measurable X₁ ∧ Measurable X₂ ∧ Measurable Y' ∧ CondIndepFun X₁ X₂ Y' ν ∧ IdentDistrib (⟨X₁, Y'⟩) (⟨X, Y⟩) ν μ ∧ IdentDistrib (⟨X₂, Y'⟩) (⟨X, Y⟩) ν μ
docstring: For `X, Y` random variables, there exist conditionally independent trials `X_1, X_2, Y'`.

name: condIndep_copies'
signature: (X : Ω → α) (Y : Ω → β) (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) [FiniteRange Y] (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] (p : α → β → Prop) (hp : Measurable (uncurry p)) (hp' : ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, p (X ω) (Y ω)) : ∃ (Ω' : Type u) (_ : MeasurableSpace Ω') (X₁ X₂ : Ω' → α) (Y' : Ω' → β) (ν : Measure Ω'), IsProbabilityMeasure ν ∧ Measurable X₁ ∧ Measurable X₂ ∧ Measurable Y' ∧ CondIndepFun X₁ X₂ Y' ν ∧ IdentDistrib (⟨X₁, Y'⟩) (⟨X, Y⟩) ν μ ∧ IdentDistrib (⟨X₂, Y'⟩) (⟨X, Y⟩) ν μ ∧ (∀ ω, p (X₁ ω) (Y' ω)) ∧ (∀ ω, p (X₂ ω) (Y' ω))
docstring: For `X, Y` random variables, there exist conditionally independent trials `X₁, X₂, Y'`.

name: finrank
signature: (s : Set P) : ℕ
docstring: The dimension of the affine span over `ℤ` of a subset of an additive group.

name: finrank_vadd_set
signature: (s : Set P) (v : V) : finrank k (v +ᵥ s) = AffineSpace.finrank k s

name: finrank_empty
signature: [Nontrivial k] : finrank k (∅ : Set P) = 0

name: finrank_le_submoduleFinrank
signature: [Module.Finite k S] (q : P) (hs : ∀ p ∈ s, p -ᵥ q ∈ S) : finrank k s ≤ S.finrank

name: finrank_le_moduleFinrank
signature: [Module.Finite k V] : finrank k s ≤ Module.finrank k V

name: setRuzsaDist
signature: (A B : Set G) : ℝ
docstring:
The Ruzsa distance between two subsets `A`, `B` of a group `G` is defined to be the Ruzsa
distance between their uniform probability distributions. Is only intended for use when `A`, `B` are
finite and non-empty.

name: setRuzsaDist_eq_rdist
signature: {A B : Set G} [Finite A] [Finite B] {Ω Ω' : Type*} [mΩ : MeasureSpace Ω] [mΩ' : MeasureSpace Ω'] {μ : Measure Ω} {μ' : Measure Ω'} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] [IsProbabilityMeasure μ'] {UA : Ω → G} {UB : Ω' → G} (hUA : IsUniform A UA μ) (hUB : IsUniform B UB μ') (hUA_mes : Measurable UA) (hUB_mes : Measurable UB) : dᵤ[A # B] = d[UA ; μ # UB ; μ']
docstring: Relating Ruzsa distance between sets to Ruzsa distance between random variables

name: setRuzsaDist_nonneg
signature: (A B : Set G) [hA : Finite A] [hB : Finite B] [Nonempty A] [Nonempty B] : 0 ≤ dᵤ[A # B]
docstring: Ruzsa distance between sets is nonnegative.

name: setRuzsaDist_symm
signature: (A B : Set G) [hA : Finite A] [hB : Finite B] [Nonempty A] [Nonempty B] : dᵤ[A # B] = dᵤ[B # A]
docstring: Ruzsa distance between sets is symmetric.

name: setRuzsaDist_triangle
signature: (A B C : Set G) [hA : Finite A] [hB : Finite B] [hC : Finite C] [Nonempty A] [Nonempty B] [Nonempty C] : dᵤ[A # C] ≤ dᵤ[A # B] + dᵤ[B # C]
docstring: Ruzsa distance between sets obeys the triangle inequality.

name: setRuzsaDist_add_const
signature: (A B : Set G) [hA : Finite A] [hB : Finite B] [Nonempty A] [Nonempty B] (c c' : G) : dᵤ[A + {c} # B + {c'}] = dᵤ[A # B]
docstring: Ruzsa distance between sets is translation invariant.

name: setRuzsaDist_of_inj
signature: (A B : Set G) [hA : Finite A] [hB : Finite B] [Nonempty A] [Nonempty B] {H : Type*} [hH : MeasurableSpace H] [MeasurableSingletonClass H] [AddCommGroup H] [Countable H] {φ : G →+ H} (hφ : Function.Injective φ) : dᵤ[φ '' A # φ '' B] = dᵤ[A # B]
docstring: Ruzsa distance between sets is preserved by injective homomorphisms.

name: setRuzsaDist_le
signature: (A B : Set G) [h'A : Finite A] [h'B : Finite B] (hA : A.Nonempty) (hB : B.Nonempty) : dᵤ[A # B] ≤ log (Nat.card (A-B)) - log (Nat.card A) / 2 - log (Nat.card B) / 2
docstring: Ruzsa distance between sets is controlled by the doubling constant.

name: max_entropy_le_entropy_add
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (h : IndepFun X Y μ) : max H[X ; μ] H[Y ; μ] ≤ H[X * Y ; μ]
docstring: If `X, Y` are independent, then `max(H[X], H[Y]) ≤ H[X * Y]`.

name: max_entropy_le_entropy_div
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (h : IndepFun X Y μ) : max H[X ; μ] H[Y ; μ] ≤ H[X / Y ; μ]
docstring: If `X, Y` are independent, then `max(H[X], H[Y]) ≤ H[X / Y]`.

name: max_entropy_le_entropy_sub
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (h : IndepFun X Y μ) : max H[X ; μ] H[Y ; μ] ≤ H[X / Y ; μ]
docstring: If `X, Y` are independent, then `max(H[X], H[Y]) ≤ H[X / Y]`.

name: max_entropy_le_entropy_prod
signature: {G : Type*} [Countable G] [hG : MeasurableSpace G] [MeasurableSingletonClass G] [CommGroup G] [MeasurableMul₂ G] {I : Type*} {s : Finset I} {i₀ : I} (hi₀ : i₀ ∈ s) {X : I → Ω → G} [∀ i, FiniteRange (X i)] (hX : (i : I) → Measurable (X i)) (h_indep : iIndepFun X μ) : H[X i₀ ; μ] ≤ H[∏ i ∈ s, X i ; μ]
docstring:
If `X₁, ..., Xₙ` are independent and `s ⊆ {1, ..., n}`, then for all `i ∈ s`,
`H[Xᵢ] ≤ H[∏ j ∈ s, Xⱼ]`.

name: max_entropy_le_entropy_sum
signature: {G : Type*} [Countable G] [hG : MeasurableSpace G] [MeasurableSingletonClass G] [CommGroup G] [MeasurableMul₂ G] {I : Type*} {s : Finset I} {i₀ : I} (hi₀ : i₀ ∈ s) {X : I → Ω → G} [∀ i, FiniteRange (X i)] (hX : (i : I) → Measurable (X i)) (h_indep : iIndepFun X μ) : H[X i₀ ; μ] ≤ H[∏ i ∈ s, X i ; μ]
docstring:
If `X₁, ..., Xₙ` are independent and `s ⊆ {1, ..., n}`, then for all `i ∈ s`,
`H[Xᵢ] ≤ H[∏ j ∈ s, Xⱼ]`.

name: entropy
signature: (X : Ω → S) (μ : Measure Ω := by volume_tac)

name: entropy_def
signature: (X : Ω → S) (μ : Measure Ω) : entropy X μ = Hm[μ.map X]
docstring: Entropy of a random variable agrees with entropy of its distribution.

name: entropy_eq_kernel_entropy
signature: (X : Ω → S) (μ : Measure Ω) : H[X ; μ] = Hk[Kernel.const Unit (μ.map X), Measure.dirac ()]
docstring: Entropy of a random variable is also the kernel entropy of the distribution over a Dirac mass.

name: entropy_zero_measure
signature: (X : Ω → S) : H[X ; (0 : Measure Ω)] = 0
docstring: Any variable on a zero measure space has zero entropy.

name: entropy_congr
signature: {X X' : Ω → S} (h : X =ᵐ[μ] X') : H[X ; μ] = H[X' ; μ]
docstring: Two variables that agree almost everywhere, have the same entropy.

name: entropy_nonneg
signature: (X : Ω → S) (μ : Measure Ω) : 0 ≤ entropy X μ
docstring: Entropy is always non-negative.

name: entropy_congr
signature: {Ω' : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω'] {μ' : Measure Ω'} {X' : Ω' → S} (h : IdentDistrib X X' μ μ') : H[X ; μ] = H[X' ; μ']
docstring: Two variables that have the same distribution, have the same entropy.

name: entropy_le_log_card
signature: [Fintype S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] (X : Ω → S) (μ : Measure Ω) : H[X ; μ] ≤ log (Fintype.card S)
docstring: Entropy is at most the logarithm of the cardinality of the range.

name: entropy_le_log_card_of_mem
signature: [DiscreteMeasurableSpace S] {A : Finset S} {μ : Measure Ω} {X : Ω → S} (hX : Measurable X) (h : ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, X ω ∈ A) : H[X ; μ] ≤ log A.card
docstring:
Entropy is at most the logarithm of the cardinality of a set in which X almost surely takes
values in.

name: entropy_le_log_card_of_mem_finite
signature: [DiscreteMeasurableSpace S] {A : Set S} {μ : Measure Ω} {X : Ω → S} (hA : A.Finite) (hX : Measurable X) (h : ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, X ω ∈ A) : H[X ; μ] ≤ log (Nat.card A)
docstring:
Entropy is at most the logarithm of the cardinality of a set in which X almost surely takes
values in.

name: entropy_eq_sum
signature: (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] : entropy X μ = ∑' x, negMulLog ((μ.map X).real {x})
docstring: `H[X] = ∑ₛ P[X=s] log 1 / P[X=s]`.

name: entropy_eq_sum'
signature: (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] : entropy X μ = ∑' x, negMulLog ((μ.map X).real {x})

name: entropy_eq_sum_finset
signature: {μ : Measure Ω} [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] {A : Finset S} (hA : (μ.map X) Aᶜ = 0) : entropy X μ = ∑ x ∈ A, negMulLog ((μ.map X).real {x})

name: entropy_eq_sum_finset'
signature: {μ : Measure Ω} [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] {A : Finset S} (hA : (μ.map X) Aᶜ = 0) : entropy X μ = ∑ x ∈ A, negMulLog ((μ.map X).real {x})

name: entropy_eq_sum_finiteRange
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass S] (hX : Measurable X) {μ : Measure Ω} [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange X] : entropy X μ = ∑ x ∈ FiniteRange.toFinset X, negMulLog ((μ.map X).real {x})

name: entropy_eq_sum_finiteRange'
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass S] (hX : Measurable X) {μ : Measure Ω} [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange X] : entropy X μ = ∑ x ∈ FiniteRange.toFinset X, negMulLog ((μ.map X).real {x})

name: entropy_cond_eq_sum
signature: (μ : Measure Ω) (y : T) : H[X | Y ← y ; μ] = ∑' x, negMulLog (((μ[|Y ← y]).map X).real {x})
docstring: `H[X | Y=y] = ∑_s P[X=s | Y=y] log 1/(P[X=s | Y=y])`.

name: entropy_cond_eq_sum_finiteRange
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass S] (hX : Measurable X) (μ : Measure Ω) (y : T) [FiniteRange X] : H[X | Y ← y ; μ] = ∑ x ∈ FiniteRange.toFinset X, negMulLog (((μ[|Y ← y]).map X).real {x})

name: entropy_comp_of_injective
signature: [MeasurableSpace T] [Countable S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] [MeasurableSingletonClass T] (μ : Measure Ω) (hX : Measurable X) (f : S → T) (hf : Function.Injective f) : H[f ∘ X ; μ] = H[X ; μ]
docstring:
If `X`, `Y` are `S`-valued and `T`-valued random variables, and `Y = f(X)` for
some injection `f : S \to T`, then `H[Y] = H[X]`.
One can also use `entropy_of_comp_eq_of_comp` as an alternative if verifying injectivity is fiddly.
For the upper bound only, see `entropy_comp_le`.

name: entropy_const
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass S] [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] (c : S) : H[fun _ ↦ c ; μ] = 0
docstring: The entropy of any constant is zero.

name: entropy_eq
signature: [DiscreteMeasurableSpace S] {H : Finset S} {X : Ω → S} {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] (hX : IsUniform H X μ) (hX' : Measurable X) : H[X ; μ] = log (Nat.card H)
docstring: If `X` is uniformly distributed on `H`, then `H[X] = log |H|`.

name: entropy_eq'
signature: [DiscreteMeasurableSpace S] {A : Set S} (hA : A.Finite) {X : Ω → S} {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] (hX : IsUniform A X μ) (hX' : Measurable X) : H[X ; μ] = log A.ncard
docstring: Variant of `IsUniform.entropy_congr` where `H` is a finite `Set` rather than `Finset`.

name: entropy_eq_log_card
signature: {X : Ω → S} [Fintype S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] (hX : Measurable X) (μ : Measure Ω) [hμ : NeZero μ] [IsFiniteMeasure μ] : entropy X μ = log (Fintype.card S) ↔ ∀ s, μ.map X {s} = μ Set.univ / Fintype.card S
docstring:
If `X` is `S`-valued random variable, then `H[X] = log |S|` if and only if `X` is uniformly
distributed.

name: prob_ge_exp_neg_entropy
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass S] (X : Ω → S) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] (hX : Measurable X) [hX' : FiniteRange X] : ∃ s : S, μ Set.univ * (rexp (- H[X ; μ])).toNNReal ≤ μ.map X {s}
docstring:
If `X` is an `S`-valued random variable, then there exists `s ∈ S` such that
`P[X = s] ≥ \exp(- H[X])`.

TODO: remove the probability measure hypothesis, which is unnecessary here.

name: prob_ge_exp_neg_entropy'
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass S] {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] (X : Ω → S) (hX : Measurable X) [FiniteRange X] : ∃ s : S, rexp (- H[X ; μ]) ≤ μ.real (X ⁻¹' {s})
docstring:
If `X` is an `S`-valued random variable, then there exists `s ∈ S` such that
`P[X=s] ≥ \exp(-H[X])`.

name: const_of_nonpos_entropy
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass S] {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] {X : Ω → S} (hX : Measurable X) [FiniteRange X] (hent : H[X; μ] ≤ 0) : ∃ s : S, μ.real (X ⁻¹' {s}) = 1
docstring:
If `X` is an `S`-valued random variable of non-positive entropy, then `X` is almost surely
constant.

name: entropy_prod_comp
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (μ : Measure Ω) (f : S → T) : H[⟨X, f ∘ X⟩; μ] = H[X ; μ]

name: entropy_comm
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) : H[⟨X, Y⟩; μ] = H[⟨Y, X⟩ ; μ]

name: entropy_assoc
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (hZ : Measurable Z) (μ : Measure Ω) : H[⟨X, ⟨Y, Z⟩⟩ ; μ] = H[⟨⟨X, Y⟩, Z⟩ ; μ]

name: condEntropy
signature: (X : Ω → S) (Y : Ω → T) (μ : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : ℝ

name: condEntropy_def
signature: (X : Ω → S) (Y : Ω → T) (μ : Measure Ω) : condEntropy X Y μ = (μ.map Y)[fun y ↦ H[X | Y ← y ; μ]]

name: condEntropy_eq_zero
signature: (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsFiniteMeasure μ] (t : T) (ht : (μ.map Y).real {t} = 0) : H[X | Y ← t ; μ] = 0

name: condEntropy_eq_kernel_entropy
signature: [Nonempty S] [Countable S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsFiniteMeasure μ] [FiniteRange Y] : H[X | Y ; μ] = Hk[condDistrib X Y μ, μ.map Y]
docstring: Conditional entropy of a random variable is equal to the entropy of its conditional kernel.

name: condEntropy_two_eq_kernel_entropy
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (hZ : Measurable Z) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange Y] [FiniteRange Z] : H[X | ⟨Y, Z⟩ ; μ] = Hk[Kernel.condKernel (condDistrib (fun a ↦ (Y a, X a)) Z μ), Measure.map Z μ ⊗ₘ Kernel.fst (condDistrib (fun a ↦ (Y a, X a)) Z μ)]

name: condEntropy_zero_measure
signature: (X : Ω → S) (Y : Ω → T) : H[X | Y ; (0 : Measure Ω)] = 0
docstring: Any random variable on a zero measure space has zero conditional entropy.

name: condEntropy_nonneg
signature: (X : Ω → S) (Y : Ω → T) (μ : Measure Ω) : 0 ≤ H[X | Y ; μ]
docstring: Conditional entropy is non-negative.

name: condEntropy_le_log_card
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass S] [Fintype S] (X : Ω → S) (Y : Ω → T) (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] : H[X | Y ; μ] ≤ log (Fintype.card S)
docstring: Conditional entropy is at most the logarithm of the cardinality of the range.

name: condEntropy_eq_sum
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass T] (X : Ω → S) (Y : Ω → T) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsFiniteMeasure μ] (hY : Measurable Y) [FiniteRange Y] : H[X | Y ; μ] = ∑ y ∈ FiniteRange.toFinset Y, ((μ.map Y).real {y}) * H[X | Y ← y ; μ]
docstring: `H[X|Y] = ∑_y P[Y=y] H[X|Y=y]`.

name: condEntropy_eq_sum_fintype
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass T] (X : Ω → S) (Y : Ω → T) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsFiniteMeasure μ] (hY : Measurable Y) [Fintype T] : H[X | Y ; μ] = ∑ y, μ.real (Y ⁻¹' {y}) * H[X | Y ← y ; μ]

name: condEntropy_prod_eq_sum
signature: {X : Ω → S} {Y : Ω → T} {Z : Ω → T'} [MeasurableSpace T'] [MeasurableSingletonClass T'] (μ : Measure Ω) (hY : Measurable Y) (hZ : Measurable Z) [IsFiniteMeasure μ] [Finite T] [Fintype T'] : H[X | ⟨Y, Z⟩ ; μ] = ∑ z, μ.real (Z ⁻¹' {z}) * H[X | Y ; μ[|Z ⁻¹' {z}]]

name: condEntropy_eq_sum_sum
signature: (hX : Measurable X) {Y : Ω → T} (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : H[X | Y ; μ] = ∑ y ∈ FiniteRange.toFinset Y, ∑ x ∈ FiniteRange.toFinset X, ((μ.map Y).real {y}) * negMulLog (((μ[|Y ← y]).map X).real {x})
docstring: `H[X|Y] = ∑_y ∑_x P[Y=y] P[X=x|Y=y] log ⧸(P[X=x|Y=y])`$.

name: condEntropy_eq_sum_sum_fintype
signature: {Y : Ω → T} (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] [Fintype S] [Fintype T] : H[X | Y ; μ] = ∑ y, ∑ x, (μ.map Y).real {y} * negMulLog (((μ[|Y ← y]).map X).real {x})
docstring: `H[X|Y] = ∑_y ∑_x P[Y=y] P[X=x|Y=y] log ⧸(P[X=x|Y=y])`$.

name: condEntropy_eq_sum_prod
signature: (hX : Measurable X) {Y : Ω → T} (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : H[X | Y ; μ] = ∑ p ∈ (FiniteRange.toFinset X) ×ˢ (FiniteRange.toFinset Y), (μ.map Y).real {p.2} * negMulLog (((μ[|Y ⁻¹' {p.2}]).map X).real {p.1})
docstring: Same as previous lemma, but with a sum over a product space rather than a double sum.

name: condEntropy_of_injective
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass U] (μ : Measure Ω) [IsFiniteMeasure μ] (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (f : T → S → U) (hf : ∀ t, Injective (f t)) [FiniteRange Y] : H[(fun ω ↦ f (Y ω) (X ω)) | Y ; μ] = H[X | Y ; μ]
docstring:
If `X : Ω → S`, `Y : Ω → T` are random variables, and `f : T × S → U` is
  injective for each fixed `t ∈ T`, then `H[f(Y, X) | Y] = H[X | Y]`.
  Thus for instance `H[X-Y|Y] = H[X|Y]`.

name: condEntropy_comp_of_injective
signature: {Y : Ω → U} (μ : Measure Ω) (hX : Measurable X) (f : S → T) (hf : Injective f) : H[f ∘ X | Y ; μ] = H[X | Y ; μ]
docstring: A weaker version of the above lemma in which `f` is independent of `Y`.

name: condEntropy_comm
signature: [Countable T] {Z : Ω → U} (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) : H[⟨X, Y⟩ | Z ; μ] = H[⟨Y, X⟩ | Z; μ]
docstring: `H[X, Y| Z] = H[Y, X| Z]`.

name: chain_rule'
signature: (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : H[⟨X, Y⟩ ; μ] = H[X ; μ] + H[Y | X ; μ]
docstring: One form of the chain rule : `H[X, Y] = H[X] + H[Y | X]`.

name: chain_rule
signature: (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : H[⟨X, Y⟩ ; μ] = H[Y ; μ] + H[X | Y ; μ]
docstring: Another form of the chain rule : `H[X, Y] = H[Y] + H[X | Y]`.

name: chain_rule''
signature: (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : H[X | Y ; μ] = H[⟨X, Y⟩ ; μ] - H[Y ; μ]
docstring: Another form of the chain rule : `H[X | Y] = H[X, Y] - H[Y]`.

name: condEntropy_eq
signature: {Ω' : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω'] {X : Ω → S} {Y : Ω → T} {μ' : Measure Ω'} {X' : Ω' → S} {Y' : Ω' → T} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] [IsProbabilityMeasure μ'] (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (hX' : Measurable X') (hY' : Measurable Y') (h : IdentDistrib (⟨X, Y⟩) (⟨X', Y'⟩) μ μ') [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] [FiniteRange X'] [FiniteRange Y'] : H[X | Y ; μ] = H[X' | Y' ; μ']
docstring:
Two pairs of variables that have the same joint distribution, have the same
conditional entropy.

name: condEntropy_of_injective'
signature: (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (f : T → U) (hf : Injective f) (hfY : Measurable (f ∘ Y)) [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : H[X | f ∘ Y ; μ] = H[X | Y ; μ]
docstring:
If `X : Ω → S` and `Y : Ω → T` are random variables, and `f : T → U` is an
injection then `H[X | f(Y)] = H[X | Y]`.

name: condEntropy_comp_self
signature: [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] (hX : Measurable X) {f : S → U} (hf : Measurable f) [FiniteRange X] : H[X | f ∘ X ; μ] = H[X ; μ] - H[f ∘ X ; μ]

name: cond_chain_rule'
signature: (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (hZ : Measurable Z) [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] [FiniteRange Z] : H[⟨X, Y⟩ | Z ; μ] = H[X | Z ; μ] + H[Y | ⟨X, Z⟩ ; μ]
docstring:
If `X : Ω → S`, `Y : Ω → T`, `Z : Ω → U` are random variables,
then `H[X, Y | Z] = H[X | Z] + H[Y|X, Z]`.

name: cond_chain_rule
signature: (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (hZ : Measurable Z) [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] [FiniteRange Z] : H[⟨X, Y⟩ | Z ; μ] = H[Y | Z ; μ] + H[X | ⟨Y, Z⟩ ; μ]
docstring: `H[X, Y | Z] = H[Y | Z] + H[X | Y, Z]`.

name: entropy_comp_le
signature: (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] (hX : Measurable X) (f : S → U) [FiniteRange X] : H[f ∘ X ; μ] ≤ H[X ; μ]
docstring:
Data-processing inequality for the entropy: `H[f(X)] ≤ H[X]`.
To upgrade this to equality, see `entropy_of_comp_eq_of_comp` or `entropy_comp_of_injective`.

name: entropy_of_comp_eq_of_comp
signature: (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (f : S → T) (g : T → S) (h1 : Y = f ∘ X) (h2 : X = g ∘ Y) [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : H[X ; μ] = H[Y ; μ]
docstring:
A Schroder-Bernstein type theorem for entropy : if two random variables are functions of each
  other, then they have the same entropy. Can be used as a substitute for
  `entropy_comp_of_injective` if one doesn't want to establish the injectivity.

name: mutualInfo
signature: (X : Ω → S) (Y : Ω → T) (μ : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : ℝ

name: mutualInfo_def
signature: (X : Ω → S) (Y : Ω → T) (μ : Measure Ω) : I[X : Y ; μ] = H[X ; μ] + H[Y ; μ] - H[⟨X, Y⟩ ; μ]

name: entropy_add_entropy_sub_mutualInfo
signature: (X : Ω → S) (Y : Ω → T) (μ : Measure Ω) : H[X ; μ] + H[Y ; μ] - I[X : Y ; μ] = H[⟨X, Y⟩ ; μ]

name: mutualInfo_eq
signature: {Ω' : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω'] {μ' : Measure Ω'} {X' : Ω' → S} {Y' : Ω' → T} (hXY : IdentDistrib (⟨X, Y⟩) (⟨X', Y'⟩) μ μ') : I[X : Y ; μ] = I[X' : Y' ; μ']
docstring:
Substituting variables for ones with the same distributions doesn't change the mutual
information.

name: condMutualInfo
signature: (X : Ω → S) (Y : Ω → T) (Z : Ω → U) (μ : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : ℝ

name: condMutualInfo_def
signature: (X : Ω → S) (Y : Ω → T) (Z : Ω → U) (μ : Measure Ω) : condMutualInfo X Y Z μ = (μ.map Z)[fun z ↦ H[X | Z ← z ; μ] + H[Y | Z ← z ; μ] - H[⟨X, Y⟩ | Z ← z ; μ]]

name: condMutualInfo_eq_integral_mutualInfo
signature: : I[X : Y | Z ; μ] = (μ.map Z)[fun z ↦ I[X : Y ; μ[| Z ⁻¹' {z}]]]

name: mutualInfo_nonneg
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : 0 ≤ I[X : Y ; μ]
docstring: Mutual information is non-negative.

name: entropy_pair_le_add
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : H[⟨X, Y⟩ ; μ] ≤ H[X ; μ] + H[Y ; μ]
docstring: Subadditivity of entropy.

name: mutualInfo_eq_zero
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) {μ : Measure Ω} [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : I[X : Y ; μ] = 0 ↔ IndepFun X Y μ
docstring: `I[X : Y] = 0` iff `X, Y` are independent.

name: mutualInfo_const
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (c : T) {μ : Measure Ω} [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange X] : I[X : fun _ ↦ c ; μ] = 0
docstring: The mutual information with a constant is always zero.

name: entropy_pair_eq_add
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) {μ : Measure Ω} [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : H[⟨X, Y⟩ ; μ] = H[X ; μ] + H[Y ; μ] ↔ IndepFun X Y μ
docstring: `H[X, Y] = H[X] + H[Y]` if and only if `X, Y` are independent.

name: entropy_eq_add
signature: {Ω S : Type*} [hΩ: MeasureSpace Ω] [IsProbabilityMeasure hΩ.volume] {m : ℕ} [MeasurableSpace S] [MeasurableSingletonClass S] [Finite S] {X : Fin m → Ω → S} (hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)) (h_indep : iIndepFun X) : H[(fun ω i ↦ X i ω)] = ∑ i, H[X i]

name: mutualInfo_comm
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) : I[X : Y ; μ] = I[Y : X ; μ]

name: mutualInfo_eq_entropy_sub_condEntropy
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : I[X : Y ; μ] = H[X ; μ] - H[X | Y ; μ]

name: mutualInfo_eq_entropy_sub_condEntropy'
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : I[X : Y ; μ] = H[Y ; μ] - H[Y | X ; μ]

name: entropy_sub_mutualInfo_eq_condEntropy
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : H[X ; μ] - I[X : Y ; μ] = H[X | Y ; μ]

name: entropy_sub_mutualInfo_eq_condEntropy'
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : H[Y ; μ] - I[X : Y ; μ] = H[Y | X ; μ]

name: condEntropy_eq_entropy
signature: {μ : Measure Ω} (h : IndepFun X Y μ) (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange X] [FiniteRange Y] : H[X | Y ; μ] = H[X ; μ]

name: condMutualInfo_eq_kernel_mutualInfo
signature: (hX : Measurable X) (hY : Measurable Y) (hZ : Measurable Z) (μ : Measure Ω) [IsZeroOrProbabilityMeasure μ] [FiniteRange Z] : I[X : Y | Z ; μ] = Ik[condDistrib (⟨X, Y⟩) Z μ, μ.map Z]
docstring: The conditional mutual information agrees with the information of the conditional kernel.

name: condMutualInfo_eq_sum
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass U] [IsFiniteMeasure μ] (hZ : Measurable Z) [FiniteRange Z] : I[X : Y | Z ; μ] = ∑ z ∈ FiniteRange.toFinset Z, μ.real (Z ⁻¹' {z}) * I[X : Y ; μ[|Z ← z]]

name: condMutualInfo_eq_sum'
signature: [MeasurableSingletonClass U] [IsFiniteMeasure μ] (hZ : Measurable Z) [Fintype U] : I[X : Y | Z ; μ] = ∑ z, μ.real (Z ⁻¹' {z}) * I[X : Y ; (μ[|Z ← z])]
